hero-description: Computer Engineer. Senior Software Engineer at Adobe since 2016. I build high-performance web solutions for ecommerce and backend platforms. Programmer, gamer, 8-bit retro fan, rock lover, and proud Galician. Based in Barcelona.
hero-focus: These days, I'm especially focused on web performance, observability, and software architecture. I'm currently part of Adobe Commerce, building Payment Services.
exp-adobe-description: Adobe is a global leader in creative and digital experience software. Within Adobe Commerce, I currently work in a team building Payment Services, improving the payment experience for merchants and customers. Adobe acquired Magento in August 2018, and since then its open-commerce innovation has been integrated into the Adobe Experience Cloud ecosystem.
exp-magento-description: Magento is an open-source ecommerce platform used to build all kinds of online commerce projects. Thanks to its modular architecture, it is highly flexible and scalable. Magento offered two product lines: Magento Open Source (formerly Community Edition) and Magento Commerce.
exp-cmp-description: Mad Collective is a Barcelona-based group of digital businesses. As Software Engineer, I worked on backend and product architecture, handling integrations, deployments, and continuous improvements in performance and code quality.
exp-softonic-description: Web development at multinational Softonic (Barcelona HQ). High-performance web engineering using agile practices (SCRUM framework). Specialized in PHP and MySQL. Worked with version control, caching systems, design patterns, unit and integration testing, and development workflows across devel, prod, test, and stress environments. Also worked as a code reviewer.
exp-softonic-trainee-description: I joined a training program at Softonic (Barcelona HQ) aimed at helping developers with around one year of prior experience become specialists in high-performance web development. The program included preparation in MySQL, Design Patterns, SCRUM methodology, and caching systems.
exp-elogia-description: Elogia is an online marketing agency. I developed and integrated social network applications for brands such as Viceroy, Mercedes-Benz, HP, Pizza Móvil, and CC Gran Vía 2. I also installed, configured, and managed WordPress websites used as corporate blogs for clients like Zelnova, Banco Mediolanum, D.O. Rías Baixas, and Qualita. I built an internal platform, the Social Media Objectives Framework, and worked on the marketing4ecommerce blog. I also contributed as an author to the company's blog.
exp-tuglabweb-description: TUGlabWEB is an interface for transferable utility games that allows calculations over the characteristic function of 3- or 4-player coalition games in the context of Game Theory. We implemented a full admin panel, user management, result persistence, and many other features. The project was developed in collaboration with GWAI (Intelligence Agent Group of University of Vigo), SAGATH (Santiago Game Theory Group), the Mathematics Department of Vigo, and SIDOR (Statistical Inference, Decisions and Operations Research).
exp-uvigo-description: I worked as an intern in three different departments at UVIGO. First at FCETOU (Faculty of Tourism and Business, Ourense), and then at FCOU (Faculty of Sciences, Ourense), focused on IT support and maintenance. In 2010 I joined UVIGO Multimedia Services and videoconferencing systems at tv.uvigo.es, working on maintenance, monitoring, and setup of AG (Access Grid) and videoconference systems (Ipela, Polycom, Adobe Connect), plus event production/post-production, room audio, recording, and processing.
act-lasalle-title: University Professor at La Salle (Ramon Llull University).
act-lasalle-description: Associate Professor at La Salle BCN. Teaching in the lifelong master's program in Software Development and Architecture, focused on backend and performance topics, while mentoring final projects. I also collaborated at La Salle between 2017 and 2022 in MPWAR.
act-geekshubs-description: Instructor at GeeksHubs, helping shape education and tech recruitment. Teacher in the DevOps Bootcamp, designed for experienced professionals who want to strengthen their skills in DevOps, architecture, and IT infrastructure.
act-codely-description: Instructor at Codely.tv Pro. Practical, no-nonsense online courses for developers taught by experienced professionals. Creator of courses such as ELK + Beats: Centralize Your Logs with Elastic Stack, Monitoring with Prometheus, and Visualize Prometheus with Grafana.
act-openwebinars-description: Content creator at OpenWebinars, focused on Go and software development best practices. Courses and workshops such as TDD in Go, BDD in Go, and WebAssembly in Go.
act-rekomendo-description: Rekomendo! was a cultural recommendation app to discover books, video games, music, board games, films, and series you would probably enjoy. You could rate content and send personalized recommendations to friends, while keeping your own rating history so you would always have suggestions ready. It was available for Android and iOS.
std-comp-science-degree-description: I obtained my Computer Engineering degree at the University of Vigo's School of Computer Engineering (ESEI), Ourense campus, Galicia.
std-comp-science-tech-title: Technical Degree in Management Information Systems.
std-comp-science-tech-description: I obtained my Technical Engineering degree in Management Information Systems at the University of Vigo's School of Computer Engineering (ESEI), Ourense campus, Galicia.
nano-gameboy-description: Turn your photos into a classic Game Boy style with retro 8-bit palettes.
nano-iw-description: Score calculator to speed up matches and avoid manual counting mistakes.
nano-emoji-description: Visual trainer to test memory and speed using emoji-based sequences.
nano-mochilo-description: Small ZX Spectrum tribute with retro visuals and 8-bit nostalgia.
nano-rekomendo-description: Rekomendo! was a cultural recommender to discover books, videogames, music, board games, movies, and series you might like.
